title: Introduction / Basics
description: What is Chemistry? Matter, elements, compounds, mixtures, states of matter, measurement, and scientific method.
references: Chemistry: The Central Science by Brown et al.
General Chemistry I & II by Zumdahl

title: Atomic Structure
description: Atomic theory history, subatomic particles, atomic number, isotopes, electron configuration, quantum numbers, and periodic trends.

title: Periodic Table
description: Organization of the periodic table, groups & periods, metals/non-metals/metalloids, specific element groups, and periodic trends.

title: Chemical Bonding
description: Ionic, covalent, polar/non-polar, metallic bonds, intermolecular forces, and molecular shapes (VSEPR).
references: Chemistry: The Molecular Nature of Matter by Silberberg

title: Stoichiometry & Chemical Reactions
description: Balancing equations, mole concept, empirical/molecular formulas, types of reactions, limiting reactants, and percent yield.

title: Acids, Bases, and pH
description: Arrhenius, Brønsted-Lowry, Lewis acids/bases, strong vs weak acids/bases, pH/pOH, buffers, and titration.
references: Principles of Chemistry by Atkins & Jones

title: Thermodynamics
description: Energy, heat, work, enthalpy, entropy, Gibbs free energy, exothermic/endothermic reactions, spontaneity, and Hess's law.

title: Kinetics & Chemical Equilibrium
description: Reaction rates, factors affecting rates, rate laws, dynamic equilibrium, and Le Chatelier's principle.

title: Redox Reactions & Electrochemistry
description: Oxidation/reduction, oxidizing/reducing agents, electrochemical cells, and standard electrode potentials.

title: Organic Chemistry
description: Introduction to organic molecules, functional groups, IUPAC nomenclature, alkanes/alkenes/alkynes, isomerism, and common reactions.
references: Organic Chemistry by Paula Y. Bruice

title: Solutions & Colligative Properties
description: Solubility, saturation, concentration units, Raoult's law, freezing point depression, and boiling point elevation.

title: Nuclear Chemistry
description: Radioactivity (α, β, γ), half-life, decay calculations, nuclear reactions, fission, and fusion.

title: Analytical Chemistry & Instrumental Methods
description: Gravimetric/volumetric analysis, spectroscopy (UV-Vis, IR, NMR), chromatography (TLC, GC, HPLC).
references: Quantitative Chemical Analysis by Daniel Harris
